marketState: neutral - fairly valued front month with genuinely conflicting cross-currents
summary: Price ($2.89) sits almost exactly at modeled fair value ($2.92, band $2.42-$3.42), so there is little edge from valuation alone. Storage is bearish (+180 Bcf surplus vs 5y, a +22 Bcf bearish surprise vs forecast) and seasonality is soft (price running -15.1% YTD vs a -6.9% median, only 38% odds of being higher in 3-4 weeks, -1.4% typical 4-week move), but seasonality reliability is explicitly labeled Low (59pp spread). Working against the bearish case: both near- and mid-term weather demand run well above the 5-year normal (+9.6% and +15.1%, trending +12% above normal) and managed money is net short at just the 8th percentile of its 5-year range - an unusually crowded short that raises squeeze risk on any bullish catalyst. Technicals are split by timeframe: 5m/15m are bullish (fresh intraday bounce) while 4h/1d/1w show a bearish structure (50<200 EMA, negative MACD on daily/weekly), which reads as a short-covering bounce inside a longer downtrend rather than a clean reversal. Net: no high-conviction directional edge either way right now.

longTerm.rationale: Storage surplus (+180 Bcf vs 5y, bearish surprise) and soft seasonality (price -15.1% YTD vs -6.9% median, only 38% prob higher in 3-4wks, -1.4% typical 4-week move) argue bearish, but elevated/rising weather demand (+9.6%/+15.1% vs 5y) and an extremely crowded managed-money short (5y percentile 8) argue against pressing shorts, while fair value is roughly balanced (front spread only -$0.032 to -$0.147 across the curve, all within band). Low seasonality reliability (59pp spread) means neither side has a real statistical edge yet.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for either: (a) a sustained close below ~$2.60-2.70 (toward the fair-value band low) alongside another bearish storage surprise, confirming the bearish case for a SHORT; or (b) a bullish storage surprise / colder forecast shift plus signs of short-covering (COT percentile rising), confirming a LONG toward the $3.00-3.40 fair-value band as winter premium builds in the curve (Dec26 $3.74, Jan27 $4.16).

shortTerm.sizing: 0.5R approx 0.5% account risk (low conviction, conflicting higher-timeframe trend)
shortTerm.rationale: Near-term weather demand is running hot (+9.6% vs 5y, upper third of range) and managed money is historically crowded short (8th percentile), a setup prone to short-covering bounces; intraday technicals (5m/15m) already show that bounce with RSI/MACD/EMA all bullish, and the front-Sep curve is only mildly backwardated (-$0.027), consistent with near-term tightness. Targets sit at the plus7D/plus14D fair-value marks ($2.94/$2.96).
shortTerm.invalidation: A break and close below $2.83 that reasserts the 4h/1d/1w downtrend (50<200 EMA, negative daily/weekly MACD) would invalidate the bounce thesis and favor the bearish storage/seasonality case instead.

Weather demand elevated above 5y normal both 1-7D (+9.6%) and 8-16D (+15.1%), trending +12% above normal
Managed money net short at just the 8th percentile of 5y range - crowded short, squeeze risk
Technical split: bullish intraday (5m/15m) bounce vs bearish daily/weekly downtrend (50<200 EMA, negative MACD)
risks: Seasonality reliability is explicitly Low (59pp historical spread) - the seasonal signal could easily fail either direction
